evidence_quotes.quote: video games that require an online connection to the publishers' servers.

paragraphs.text: While I agree with the aims of the Digital Fairness Act, I feel like another addendum should be made to it; namely regarding video games that require an online connection to the publishers' servers. Currently, publishers are not required to have an end-of-life plan for their games, so what is likely to happen (and has already happened to hundreds of games) is that when the publisher ends support for such a game and their servers are shut down, the game (the consumer has already paid for, mind you!) will become non-functional, possibly forever. The Stop Destroying Video Games European Citizens' Initiative aimed at putting a stop to this practice has already gathered over 1.4 million signatures, so this problem definitely affects EU citizens across the Union and many people in Europe want to see change in this area. Video games are unique pieces of our culture in the 21st century, each game one of a kind that can't simply be replaced with another. Therefore, I believe that provisions aimed at preventing this business practice in the video game industry should also be made part of the Digital Fairness Act.
